trivia_bullets.text: Triton Wavebreaker places a Theros creature type — Triton, the amphibious merfolk who worship sea-god Thassa — into the Modern Horizons direct-to-Modern slot. Tritons are mechanically classed as Merfolk in the rules (type_line: Enchantment Creature — Merfolk Wizard). In Theros lore, Tritons are partially amphibious, revere Thassa above all other gods, and are characterized by large hook-shaped fins at the back of the head.
sources: WebSearch: MTG Wiki — Triton / Merfolk / Thassa lore

trivia_bullets.text: Bestow is a keyword ability introduced in Theros (2013), tied to that set's god-and-enchantment theme. It allows an enchantment creature to be cast as an Aura; if the enchanted creature leaves the battlefield, the Bestow card becomes a creature again rather than going to the graveyard. MH3 is one of Bestow's rare non-Theros appearances — its prior returns were Commander 2018 and MH3. The combination with Prowess is new to MH3: no Theros-block bestow card had Prowess on either face.
sources: WebSearch: CoolStuffInc — Magic Mechanics Overview: Bestow; Wizards — Theros Mechanics 2013-09-02

trivia_bullets.text: At {U} for a 1/1 with Prowess (as a creature) or Bestow {1}{U} to grant +1/+1 and Prowess to any creature, this is a 1-drop tempo piece in blue spell-heavy strategies. In Modern, primary use as a Prowess enabler — either a standalone body that grows on each noncreature spell, or an Aura that passes Prowess to a larger threat. Not Pauper-legal. EDHREC rank: 12,534.
Illustrated by Christina Kraus. No flavor text. Previewed on May 23, 2024, on the Wizards of the Coast card image gallery for MH3.
sources: Scryfall: MH3-74 artist, flavor_text absent, preview field — source: Wizards of the Coast, source_uri: magic.wizards.com/en/products/modern-horizons-3/card-image-gallery, previewed_at: 2024-05-23
